judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are substantively equivalent, correctly identifying the root cause, the introducing commit, refuting the flaky-model conclusion with the same score_one.py argument, and assessing the rollback plan identically; report 2 is slightly more precise with line-number citations but this doesn't change coverage of expectations.

report_1.evidence: The root cause is a stale config key in `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`. Its `expected_label_field` is set to `label`, but the eval-set schema was migrated so that every record in `ticket-scorer/fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` now stores its gold answer under `gold_label`... Fix: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label` in `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`.

report_2.evidence: The config declares `expected_label_field: label` (`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml:2`), but every record in the eval set now stores its gold label under the key `gold_label`... The fix is a one-line change to `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`, setting `expected_label_field: gold_label`.

report_1.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, introduced by commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"). Evidence: `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.91 on 2026-06-16 and exactly 0.00 on every date from 2026-06-17 onward...

report_2.evidence: Commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"), authored 2026-06-17... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.91 on 2026-06-16 and 0.00 starting exactly 2026-06-17 — the same date as this commit

report_1.evidence: `score_one.py` (used for all 20 spot-checks) only calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the label — it never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never computes `expected_label_field`, and never compares against any gold value. ... Correct reproduction: run the actual nightly batch entry point, `pipeline/score_batch.py`

report_2.evidence: it re-ran tickets through `pipeline/score_one.py`, which... never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never reads `expected_label_field`, and never compares against any gold label. It is structurally incapable of exercising the code path where the bug lives... A correct reproduction must exercise the actual nightly path, `pipeline/score_batch.py::score()`

report_1.evidence: Acting on this would burn a deploy/rollback cycle, delay the actual one-line config fix, and — because it changes nothing relevant to the bug — accuracy would stay at 0.00 afterward, likely reinforcing the incorrect "flaky model" narrative rather than correcting it.

report_2.evidence: Rolling back "the model" changes nothing about the `expected_label_field`/`gold_label` mismatch that actually causes the 0.0 score, so the team would burn a deploy/rollback cycle, very likely still observe 0.0 accuracy afterward
